title: Praise the lord—the iPhone 15 just got a little more affordable in Nepal!
summary: If you are looking for an affordable non-Pro iPhone, the iPhone 15 is probably still the way to go for most folks in Nepal. Its price has been in a constant swing—sometimes going high, sometimes staying low. You just never know. And right now, the iPhone 15 is available with a flat NPR 12,000 cashback in Nepal. So, in this article, let's walk through the specs, features, and the latest official pr
content:
If you are looking for an affordable non-Pro iPhone, the iPhone 15 is probably still the way to go for most folks in Nepal. Its price has been in a constant swing—sometimes going high, sometimes staying low. You just never know. And right now, the iPhone 15 is available with a flat NPR 12,000 cashback in Nepal. So, in this article, let's walk through the specs, features, and the latest official price in Nepal of the iPhone 15. Advertisement iPhone 15 Overview: Design and Display The iPhone 15 shares an identical visual to the iPhone 14 . While the titanium frame is reserved for the Pro/Pro Max siblings, the aerospace-grade aluminum frame is still a sturdy thing on the vanilla iPhone 15. Besides this, its rear panel is polished with nanocrystalline particles for a textured matte finish. Likewise, you get an IP68 rating for protection against dust and water drips. Apple ships this guy in five color options: Black, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Pink. Getting to the display, it's the same panel as the iPhone 14 Pro at 6.1 inches in size. This Super Retina XDR screen bears an FHD+ resolution (2556 x 1179 pixels), HDR10, Dolby Vision, 2,000 nits of peak brightness, and Ceramic Shield glass protection. Sadly, the refresh rate has stayed the same at 60Hz to bridge the gap with the Pro siblings. What's new this time is the arrival of Dynamic Island, Apple's "innovative" way of making the best out of a notch, introduced in the iPhone 14 Pro Series. And this finally marks the end of the gigantic notch. A16 Bionic Running the show on the core of the iPhone 15 is Apple's last year's chipset, the A16 Bionic . To recall, Apple brought the culture of using last-gen chips to the vanilla and Plus models from the iPhone 14 series. While it's not as powerful as the latest A17 Pro SoC on the iPhone 15 Pro lineup, the A16 Bionic still packs a load of punch. This 4nm-fabbed chip features a hexa-core architecture with two Everest cores at 3.46GHz and four Sawtooth cores at 2.0GHz clock speeds. Handling the graphics tasks and gaming is the 5-core GPU from Apple. As for the software, the iPhone 15 ran on iOS 17 at launch, but Apple has already pushed the iOS 26 update for this guy. Which brings the fairly controversial “Liquid Glass” visual makeover, besides everything else. Camera Getting to the optics, Apple still went with a dual camera setup on this vanilla model. However, the primary shooter gets a resolution bump this time, with a 48MP sensor. This quad-pixel camera brings 100% Focus Pixels for swift autofocus. What's more interesting is the 24MP super-high-res mode, which is set by default. Apple claims superb image quality at a practical file size with this mode. To address the lack of a zoom unit here, this vanilla model also offers a 2X telephoto option. Moving to the front, you get a 12MP TrueDepth camera engraved on the Dynamic Island, which also doubles as a 3D face scanner for biometric security. USB-C and others With the EU mandating a USB-C port in different consumer electronic goods in its region, Apple has finally abandoned its Lightning port on the entire iPhone 15 series. Unfortunately, the vanilla model equips an older, slower USB 2.0 port while the Pro siblings enjoy a faster USB 3.0 connection. You still get reverse charging via a USB-C to USB-C cable to charge your new updated AirPods Pro and Apple Watch. As usual, Apple hasn't mentioned the actual battery size of its latest iPhones, but unofficial third-party teardowns should reveal that info shortly. Anyway, there's no upgrade in the charging department since the iPhone 15's wired and wireless charging speed is still capped at 20W and 15W (via MagSafe), respectively. Whereas the phone still retains satellite connectivity, crash detection, and emergency SOS features. Apple has also introduced "Roadside Assistance" this time, which uses satellite connection for calling in help for vehicles in deserted spots with no cellular connectivity. iPhone 15 Specifications: Design, build: 147.6 x 71.6 x 7.8 mm, 171 gm, IP68 rating Display: 6.1-inch OLED screen, FHD+, 60Hz refresh rate, Ceramic Shield protection Chipset: A16 Bionic (4nm mobile platform) Memory: 6GB RAM, Up to 512GB storage (non-expandable) Software & UI: iOS 17 (upgradable to iOS 26) Rear Camera: Dual (48MP main, 12MP ultrawide) Front Camera: 12MP (Dynamic Island) Security: Face ID (via the front camera) Connectivity: Dual-SIM (Nano + eSIM), WiFi 6 (dual band), Bluetooth 5.3, GPS / Glonass / Galileo / BeiDou / QZSS, USB-C, NFC, 4G LTE, 5G Sensors: Accelerometer, Barometer, Dual ambient light, Gyroscope, Proximity Battery: 3349mAh with 20W charging (no power adapter provided) iPhone 15 Price in Nepal and Availability The latest official price of the iPhone 15 in Nepal is now NPR 110,999 for the sole 128GB model after a flat NPR 12,000 cashback. You'll also get a complimentary one-time breakage insurance and a 20W Apple original adapter for free if you buy an iPhone 15 in Nepal from Apple-authorized stores. iPhone 15 Price in Nepal (Official) 128GB NPR 122,999 , 110,999 VIDEO

Besides this, its rear panel is polished with nanocrystalline particles for a textured matte finish. Likewise, you get an IP68 rating for protection against dust and water drips. Apple ships this guy in five color options: Black, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Pink.

at 6.1 inches in size. This Super Retina XDR screen bears an FHD+ resolution (2556 x 1179 pixels), HDR10, Dolby Vision, 2,000 nits of peak brightness, and Ceramic Shield glass protection. Sadly, the refresh rate has stayed the same at 60Hz to bridge the gap with the Pro siblings. What's new this time is the arrival of Dynamic Island, Apple's "innovative" way of making the best out of a notch, introduced in the iPhone 14 Pro Series. And this finally marks the end of the gigantic notch.

Running the show on the core of the iPhone 15 is Apple's last year's chipset, the A16 Bionic . To recall, Apple brought the culture of using last-gen chips to the vanilla and Plus models from the iPhone 14 series. While it's not as powerful as the latest A17 Pro SoC on the iPhone 15 Pro lineup, the A16 Bionic still packs a load of punch. update for this guy. Which brings the fairly controversial “Liquid Glass” visual makeover, besides everything else.

Getting to the optics, Apple still went with a dual camera setup on this vanilla model. However, the primary shooter gets a resolution bump this time, with a 48MP sensor. This quad-pixel camera brings 100% Focus Pixels for swift autofocus. What's more interesting is the 24MP super-high-res mode, which is set by default. Apple claims superb image quality at a practical file size with this mode. To address the lack of a zoom unit here, this vanilla model also offers a 2X telephoto option.

Moving to the front, you get a 12MP TrueDepth camera engraved on the Dynamic Island, which also doubles as a 3D face scanner for biometric security.

With the EU mandating a USB-C port in different consumer electronic goods in its region, Apple has finally abandoned its Lightning port on the entire iPhone 15 series. Whereas the phone still retains satellite connectivity, crash detection, and emergency SOS features. Apple has also introduced "Roadside Assistance" this time, which uses satellite connection for calling in help for vehicles in deserted spots with no cellular connectivity.

The latest official price of the iPhone 15 in Nepal is now NPR 110,999 for the sole 128GB model after a flat NPR 12,000 cashback. You'll also get a complimentary one-time breakage insurance and a 20W Apple original adapter for free if you buy an iPhone 15 in Nepal from Apple-authorized stores.
